name: Laminar
tagline: Open-source, OpenTelemetry-native tracing and evals for AI agents
seoTitle: Laminar - Open-Source LLM Agent Tracing & Evals
metaDescription: Laminar is an open-source observability platform for AI agents: OpenTelemetry tracing, evals, AI monitoring, and SQL access to all trace data. Written in Rust.
overview: Laminar is an open-source observability platform built specifically for AI agents. It collects traces from your LLM and agent code, lets you run evaluations, monitor behavior, and query everything as data. The backend is written in Rust for high-throughput trace ingestion and realtime viewing.

It is aimed at developers building agents and LLM apps who need to see what their code actually did at each step. Its tracing SDK is OpenTelemetry-native, so a single line of setup auto-instruments common frameworks and providers including the Vercel AI SDK, Browser Use, Stagehand, LangChain, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini.

As an LLM observability and tracing tool, Laminar covers the full loop: capture traces, define natural-language events to flag issues, run evals locally or in CI/CD, and query traces, metrics, and events through a built-in SQL editor. You can use the managed platform at laminar.sh or self-host the stack with Docker Compose.
features: OpenTelemetry-native SDK that auto-traces Vercel AI SDK, Browser Use, Stagehand, LangChain, OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini and more with one line of code
Evals: an extensible SDK and CLI to run evaluations locally or in CI/CD, plus a UI for visualizing and comparing results
AI monitoring via natural-language event descriptions to track issues, logical errors, and custom agent behavior
SQL access to all data through a built-in SQL editor, with bulk dataset creation from queries and API access
Custom dashboards for traces, metrics, and events, including custom SQL queries
Rust backend with a realtime engine, full-text search over span data, and a gRPC exporter

gettingStarted.intro: Use the managed platform at laminar.sh, or self-host with Docker Compose. After creating a project and API key, add the SDK in TypeScript or Python.

steps.heading: Self-host with Docker Compose (optional)
steps.body: Clone the repo and start the services. The UI is available at http://localhost:5667. If you self-host, configure the SDK with the matching baseUrl and ports.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/lmnr-ai/lmnr
cd lmnr
docker compose up -d

steps.heading: Install the TypeScript SDK
steps.body: This installs the Laminar TS SDK and all instrumentation packages. Create a project and generate a project API key first.

steps.heading: Initialize and trace (TypeScript)
steps.body: Initialize Laminar with your project API key, then wrap functions with observe to trace their inputs and outputs.

steps.heading: Or use the Python SDK
steps.body: Install with the [all] extra to include all instrumentation packages, initialize Laminar, then annotate functions with the @observe() decorator.

useCases: Debug long-running AI agents by viewing each step's inputs and outputs as traces appear in realtime
Run evaluations on LLM and agent outputs locally or in a CI/CD pipeline and compare results in the UI
Monitor production agents by defining natural-language events that flag logical errors and custom behavior
Query traces, metrics, and events with SQL to build dashboards or bulk-create datasets from real usage
